task_completion.description: Verify the agent imported the Ghibli scene image into the animation software.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenToonz interface (or similar animation software) is visible with the Spirited Away tunnel scene loaded in the viewer, timeline, or xsheet.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent applied a horizontal camera pan to the right.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The camera pegbar, animation timeline, or function editor shows keyframes for horizontal translation (X-axis), or the viewer shows the camera frame shifted to the right relative to the base image.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent added sparkling particles that float upwards.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX schematic or timeline shows a particle effect node/layer, and the viewer shows small particle elements (sparkles) added to the scene.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent applied a wave distortion effect to the bottom portion of the image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The FX schematic shows a distortion, warp, or wave effect node, and the viewer shows the bottom third of the image being distorted or wavy.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent exported the final animation to the specified output directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The render/export dialog shows the output path set to /home/ga/OpenToonz/task/agent_output/, or a file manager/terminal shows the newly exported video file in that exact directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical interface of the animation software to create the effects.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenToonz application open and being interacted with (e.g., adjusting FX nodes, keyframes), rather than the agent exclusively using terminal commands like ffmpeg or python scripts to generate the video.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not simply copy the reference video to the output directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal or file manager does not show a 'cp' command copying /home/ga/OpenToonz/task/reference_animation.mp4 directly to the agent_output directory.

integrity.description: Verify the exported animation was actually rendered from the project.
integrity.visual_evidence: A rendering progress bar, render queue, or the output settings dialog is visible, showing the export process being initiated from within the animation software.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The base image is the final tunnel scene from Spirited Away. The reference animation is exactly 60 frames long at 30 FPS. It features a horizontal camera pan to the right, sparkling particles floating upwards, and a sine wave distortion applied to the bottom third of the image. The expected output directory is /home/ga/OpenToonz/task/agent_output/.
